zotero.abstractNote: Layer Normalization (LN) is a critical component in Transformer architectures. Existing variants, primarily Pre-LN and Post-LN, exhibit a fundamental trade-off: Pre-LN is stable for training but often underperforms in terms of final model quality, while Post-LN achieves better performance but suffers from training instability, especially in deeper models. In this paper, we propose Mix-LN, a novel normalization strategy that dynamically combines the benefits of both Pre-LN and Post-LN. By integrating a learnable mixing mechanism, Mix-LN allows the model to adaptively leverage the stability of Pre-LN in early layers and the superior performance of Post-LN in deeper layers. We provide theoretical insights into why this combination is effective and conduct extensive experiments across various tasks, including language modeling and vision tasks. Our results demonstrate that Mix-LN consistently outperforms both Pre-LN and Post-LN, enabling the training of significantly deeper models without sacrificing stability or performance.
